---
title: "outlier_sim"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(broom)

outlier_rcbd1 <- function(blocks, treatments,
                         beta_b, sigma_e,
                         sigma_G, pi) {

  data <- expand.grid(
    block = factor(1:blocks),
    treatment = factor(1:treatments)
  )

  alpha_t <- rep(0, treatments)

  n <- nrow(data)

  outlier <- runif(n) < pi

  e <- rnorm(n, 0, sigma_e)
  e[outlier] <- rnorm(sum(outlier), 0, sigma_G)

  data$response <-
    alpha_t[as.integer(data$treatment)] +
    beta_b[as.integer(data$block)] +
    e

  model <- aov(response ~ treatment + block, data = data)

  p_value <- anova(model) |>
    broom::tidy() |>
    dplyr::filter(term == "treatment") |>
    dplyr::pull(p.value)

  p_value < 0.05
}
```

```{r}
set.seed(100)

beta_b <- c(-5, -3, -1, 0, 1, 3, 5)

pi_grid <- seq(0, 0.1, by = 0.01)

outlier_pi_curve <- tibble(pi_vals = pi_grid) |>
  mutate(t1er = map_dbl(pi_vals, function(pi) {
    map_lgl(1:5000, ~ outlier_rcbd1(7, 8, beta_b, 4, 100, pi)) |>
        mean()
      }))
```

```{r}
ggplot(outlier_pi_curve,
       aes(x = pi_vals, y = t1er)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  labs(
    x = expression(pi),
    y = "Type I Error Rate",
    title = "Type I Error Rate for Data With Extreme Outliers"
  ) +
  ylim(0, 0.1) +
  theme_bw()
```

```{r}
set.seed(100)

pi_grid1 <- seq(0, 1, by = 0.05)

outlier_pi_curve1 <- tibble(pi_vals = pi_grid1) |>
  mutate(t1er = map_dbl(pi_vals, function(pi) {
    map_lgl(1:5000, ~ outlier_rcbd1(7, 8, beta_b, 4, 100, pi)) |>
        mean()
      }))
```

```{r}
ggplot(outlier_pi_curve1,
       aes(x = pi_vals, y = t1er)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  labs(
    x = expression(pi),
    y = "Type I Error Rate",
    title = "Type I Error Rate for Data With Extreme Outliers"
  ) +
  ylim(0, 0.1) +
  theme_bw()
```

```{r}
outlier_rcbd2 <- function(blocks, treatments,
                         beta_b, sigma_e, pi) {

  data <- expand.grid(
    block = factor(1:blocks),
    treatment = factor(1:treatments)
  )

  alpha_t <- rep(0, treatments)

  n <- nrow(data)

  outlier <- runif(n) < pi

  e <- rnorm(n, 0, sigma_e)
  e[outlier] <- rep(100, sum(outlier))

  data$response <-
    alpha_t[as.integer(data$treatment)] +
    beta_b[as.integer(data$block)] +
    e

  model <- aov(response ~ treatment + block, data = data)

  p_value <- anova(model) |>
    broom::tidy() |>
    dplyr::filter(term == "treatment") |>
    dplyr::pull(p.value)

  p_value < 0.05
}
```

```{r}
set.seed(100)

outlier_pi_curve2 <- tibble(pi_vals = pi_grid) |>
  mutate(t1er = map_dbl(pi_vals, function(pi) {
    map_lgl(1:5000, ~ outlier_rcbd2(7, 8, beta_b, 4, pi)) |>
        mean()
      }))
```

```{r}
ggplot(outlier_pi_curve2,
       aes(x = pi_vals, y = t1er)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  labs(
    x = expression(pi),
    y = "Type I Error Rate",
    title = "Type I Error Rate for Data With Extreme Outliers"
  ) +
  ylim(0, 0.1) +
  theme_bw()
```
